variants.subject: Your FMCSA safety score
variants.body:
Hey {{firstName}},

Looked up {{companyName}} on the FMCSA Safety Measurement System — your score is sitting in the alert zone.

One more bad inspection and you're looking at a compliance review or a $16,550 citation.

I built FileFlo for carriers in exactly this situation. It tracks all 85+ required documents automatically and flags anything coming due before an inspector shows up.

Worth a 10-minute look?

{{senderFirstName}}
getfileflo.com

variants.subject: What auditors actually look for
variants.body:
Hey {{firstName}},

Most FMCSA violations come down to three things:

  • Expired or missing driver qualification files
  • HOS records that don't match logs
  • Drug and alcohol testing records out of order

FileFlo catches all three automatically and alerts you 30 days before anything expires. Takes 15 minutes to set up.

Free trial at getfileflo.com.

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: Last note
variants.body:
Hey {{firstName}},

Last email from me.

If you're managing compliance manually, you're one missed document away from a $16,550 fine. FileFlo exists to make sure that doesn't happen.

14-day free trial, no card required: getfileflo.com

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: your trucking book
variants.body:
{{firstName}},

Quick question — if one of your fleet clients got a DOT compliance review tomorrow, would you know before they did whether they'd pass?

Most brokers find out after the fact. By then the carrier's authority is at risk, the policy's in chaos, and you're fielding calls you don't have good answers to.

Worth a short conversation about how some brokers are staying ahead of that?

{{senderFirstName}}

{{firstName}},

The brokers I hear this from most are running mid-size books — 20 to 60 fleet clients. You know them all personally, but you can't realistically track every driver file, medical cert, and inspection record across all of them.

A broker I talked to recently had a client lose their operating authority over expired medical certificates. She'd been their broker for eleven years. Lost the account at renewal.

The gap wasn't the coverage. It was paperwork her client thought was handled.

The tool I built flags exactly that — before the auditor does. Would it be useful to see what it surfaces for one of your clients?

{{senderFirstName}}

{{firstName}},

Should've mentioned this earlier — we pay brokers 15% recurring on every client they bring on.

If 10 of your trucking clients sign up at $299/month, that's $450/month just sitting on top of your normal renewals. It compounds as you bring on more clients. A few brokers use it purely as a retention play — clients who track compliance with it renew more reliably because they can actually prove they're clean.

Worth seeing the partner one-pager?

{{senderFirstName}}

{{firstName}},

Last one from me — I'm not going to keep nudging.

If a trucking client ever calls you after a compliance notice and you want something fast to point them to, we're at getfileflo.com. 14-day free trial, no card required.

Good luck with the book.

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: {{firstName}}, if a DOT inspector walked in today
variants.body:
{{firstName}} —

Quick scenario: a DOT inspector walks into {{companyName}} right now and asks for proof of compliance — driver qualification files, annual inspection records, HOS logs.

How long does it take to pull everything they need?

Most carriers I talk to say anywhere from 20 minutes to "we're not sure where everything is."

FileFlo was built for that moment. It centralizes every compliance doc and lets you generate an audit-ready package in under 60 seconds.

If it's useful, I can walk you through what it would look like for {{companyName}} — just reply.

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: what {{companyName}} probably doesn't have organized
variants.body:
{{firstName}} — following up on my last note.

The thing I hear most before carriers start using FileFlo: "We thought we were compliant — until someone actually asked for the proof."

The gaps are almost always the same: driver qualification files, annual DOT inspections, vehicle maintenance records. Docs that exist somewhere, just not in one searchable place.

FileFlo organizes everything by regulation — FMCSA, OSHA, IRS, EPA — so when an inspector asks, it's there in seconds.

14 days free, no card required: getfileflo.com

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: the math on one FMCSA violation
variants.body:
{{firstName}} — one more thought.

FMCSA violations average over $16,000 per incident — often for one missing or expired document.

FileFlo is $299/month. For most carriers, it pays for itself the first time it prevents a citation or helps pass a clean audit.

14-day free trial, takes about 10 minutes to set up: getfileflo.com

{{senderFirstName}}

variants.subject: last note, {{firstName}}
variants.body:
{{firstName}} — I'll stop reaching out after this.

If compliance documentation isn't a current priority, totally understood.

If you ever find yourself scrambling during a roadside inspection or DOT audit, FileFlo is at getfileflo.com — 14 days free, no card required.

Good luck out there.

{{senderFirstName}}
